_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how people's values and beliefs shape who they are, how they can differ between people, and how those differences show up in relationships.

_source.w: We are learning how people's values and beliefs shape who they are, how they can differ between people, and how those differences show up in relationships.
y: Understanding that people hold different values and beliefs helps us approach relationships with curiosity rather than judgement.
Recognising diverse perspectives prepares us for a world where we will live, work and connect with people whose views differ from our own.
This awareness helps us navigate disagreements more maturely by understanding the deeper reasons behind people's positions.
eg: We create a ranked list of values that matter most to us in a particular context — like friendship or school — and compare our lists with classmates to see similarities and differences.
We interview a parent, grandparent or carer to learn what they value most and how they show those values through their relationships.
We discuss how cultural, religious and family backgrounds shape the values people hold and how this influences their behaviour.
We explore a scenario where two people disagree because of different values and discuss how they could still maintain a respectful relationship.
We reflect on how our own values have changed as we have grown up and what experiences influenced those changes.
We research how communities with diverse values find ways to coexist and support one another, using real examples.
